title: DALL-E API Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers to effectively use the DALL-E API for image generation tasks.

commands.command: prompt="A futuristic cityscape with flying cars"
commands.description: Generate an image based on a textual description.
commands.scenario: When creating concept art for a sci-fi project.

commands.description: Specify the resolution of the generated image.
commands.scenario: When high-resolution images are needed for print media.

commands.description: Generate multiple images for a single prompt.
commands.scenario: When needing multiple variations for a design review.

commands.description: Authenticate API requests using your API key.
commands.scenario: When setting up the API for the first time.

commands.description: Receive the image data as a base64-encoded JSON string.
commands.scenario: When integrating with applications that process JSON data.

commands.description: Apply a specific artistic style to the generated image.
commands.scenario: When creating artwork that mimics historical art styles.

commands.description: Set a timeout for the API request in seconds.
commands.scenario: When handling requests in environments with strict time constraints.

commands.description: Use a specific seed for deterministic image generation.
commands.scenario: When needing reproducible results for testing.

commands.description: Define the aspect ratio of the generated image.
commands.scenario: When creating images for widescreen displays.

commands.description: Enable detailed logging for troubleshooting API requests.
commands.scenario: When diagnosing issues with API calls.

commands.description: Apply a strict content policy to filter generated images.
commands.scenario: When adhering to strict content guidelines.

commands.description: Automatically retry failed API requests up to three times.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in unstable network conditions.

commands.description: Use a vibrant color palette for the generated image.
Utilize the inpainting endpoint to modify specific regions of an existing image by providing a mask image.
Generate subtle visual variations of an uploaded image while maintaining the original composition and subject matter.
Implement attention steering by adjusting the relative importance of specific tokens within the prompt string.
Capture and log the unique 'request_id' from the response header to trace specific generation failures in high-concurrency environments.
Implement an exponential backoff algorithm for 429 (Too Many Requests) errors to optimize throughput without hitting rate limits.
